clusters.name: Technology & Cyber
clusters.description: Installing, operating, defending, and administering computer networks and information systems, and conducting offensive/defensive cyberspace operations.
aptitude_signals: Troubleshoots computers or home networks without being asked
Enjoys logic puzzles, coding, or scripting
Reads technical documentation without losing patience
Has built, modified, or repaired a PC or network setup
related_asvab_composites: Navy:no line score — per-rating subtest sums
USCG:no line score — per-rating subtest sums
clusters.notes: Marine Corps cyber MOS codes (17XX series) are less consistently documented in the sources checked this session than other branches' lists; confirm exact codes against the current MOS manual in RUN SPECIALTIES. Space Force's cyber specialty exists (it inherited cyber missions from the Air Force) but its current SFSC numbering could not be verified this session — the official spaceforce.mil SFSC crosswalk PDF returned HTTP 403 on fetch.

clusters.name: Healthcare & Medical
clusters.description: Direct patient care, emergency medicine, dentistry, and allied health support in garrison, shipboard, and field settings.
aptitude_signals: Stays calm and functional when someone is hurt or in crisis
Comfortable with bodily fluids, injuries, and clinical detail
Interested in anatomy, biology, or how the body works
Has done caregiving, babysitting, or coaching and gravitated toward the 'take care of people' role
related_asvab_composites: Navy:no line score — per-rating subtest sums
USCG:no line score — per-rating subtest sums
clusters.notes: Marine Corps has no organic enlisted medical MOS — medical care for Marines is provided by Navy Hospital Corpsmen (HM) attached to Marine units. A person who wants to do medicine embedded with Marines enlists in the Navy as a Corpsman, not in the Marine Corps. Space Force likewise has no independent medical enlisted career field; medical support is provided by Air Force personnel.

clusters.name: Aviation
clusters.description: Flying, maintaining, and supporting fixed-wing and rotary-wing aircraft, and the air traffic and flight-operations systems around them.
aptitude_signals: Drawn to how aircraft systems work, not just flying itself
Good spatial reasoning and reaction time
Comfortable with checklists and procedure-heavy work
Interested in mechanical systems combined with electronics
related_asvab_composites: Navy:no line score — per-rating subtest sums
USCG:no line score — per-rating subtest sums

Navy: AME — Aviation Structural Mechanic — Safety Equipment
Marine Corps: UNVERIFIED code — Aircraft Maintenance (60XX–63XX series, airframe-specific)

clusters.notes: Space Force has no aviation specialties — it does not operate aircraft; airlift for Space Force needs uses Air Force assets. Marine Corps aviation-maintenance MOS codes are airframe-specific and numerous (60XX–63XX range); the exact code was not confirmed this session and is flagged rather than guessed.

clusters.name: Mechanical & Engineering
clusters.description: Hands-on repair, construction, and engineering of vehicles, machinery, structures, and utility systems.
aptitude_signals: Has taken apart or fixed a car, engine, or appliance for fun
Comfortable with hand and power tools
Enjoys shop class, robotics, or DIY builds
Thinks in physical/mechanical systems
related_asvab_composites: Navy:no line score — per-rating subtest sums
USCG:no line score — per-rating subtest sums
clusters.notes: Space Force has no mechanical/construction trades of its own — facility and vehicle maintenance at Space Force installations is performed by Air Force personnel or contractors.

clusters.name: Law Enforcement & Security
clusters.description: Military police, installation/physical security, corrections, and force-protection roles.
aptitude_signals: Interested in rules, procedure, and de-escalation, not only physical confrontation
Comfortable being the one who intervenes in a conflict
Detail-oriented about reports and procedure
Has considered a civilian law-enforcement or corrections career
related_asvab_composites: Navy:no line score — per-rating subtest sums
USCG:no line score — per-rating subtest sums
clusters.notes: Space Force has no dedicated security-forces enlisted specialty; installation security at Space Force bases is provided by Air Force Security Forces personnel.

clusters.name: Logistics & Supply
clusters.description: Ordering, storing, tracking, and moving parts, supplies, fuel, and equipment; warehouse, inventory, and transportation coordination.
aptitude_signals: Likes organizing, inventory systems, or spreadsheets
Good at tracking many moving pieces without losing track
Interested in how supply chains and shipping work
Has run a stockroom or organized logistics for a group/event
related_asvab_composites: Navy:no line score — per-rating subtest sums
USCG:no line score — per-rating subtest sums
clusters.notes: Space Force logistics functions draw on the shared Air Force logistics-readiness career field rather than a fully separate, well-documented SFSC track; exact current SFSC numbering was not verified within this session's tool budget.

clusters.name: Combat Arms
clusters.description: Direct ground combat roles — infantry, armor, and field/cannon artillery — focused on close combat and fires.
aptitude_signals: Physically pushes self in sports or training
Comfortable operating as part of a small team under stress
Drawn to weapons, tactics, or field/outdoor environments
Has sought out physically and mentally demanding challenges
clusters.notes: Combat arms is concentrated almost entirely in the Army and Marine Corps. Navy, Air Force, Space Force, and Coast Guard have no equivalent ground combat-arms occupational specialty — their force-protection/security roles (Master-at-Arms, Security Forces, Maritime Enforcement Specialist) are listed under law-enforcement-security instead, since they are not infantry/armor/artillery roles.

clusters.name: Business & Administration
clusters.description: Personnel administration, finance, accounting, and records-management functions that run a unit's administrative side.
aptitude_signals: Comfortable with paperwork, recordkeeping, and process compliance
Good with numbers and budgets
Interested in HR, accounting, or office management
Has held a treasurer/organizer-type role in a club or job
related_asvab_composites: Navy:no line score — per-rating subtest sums
USCG:no line score — per-rating subtest sums

Space Force: UNVERIFIED code — administrative/financial-management support (shared Air Force career field)

clusters.notes: Space Force administrative and finance functions are largely performed by Air Force personnel in shared career fields rather than a dedicated, independently documented SFSC track; treat as thin/shared presence.

clusters.name: Culinary
clusters.description: Food service and preparation — planning menus, cooking, and running dining facilities and galleys for units, ships, and installations.
aptitude_signals: Cooks for other people regularly and enjoys it
Has worked a kitchen or food-service job
Interested in food safety, nutrition, or the culinary industry
Comfortable with fast-paced, high-volume production work
related_asvab_composites: Navy:no line score — per-rating subtest sums
USCG:no line score — per-rating subtest sums
clusters.hero_image_query: military dining facility galley food service

Air Force: 3F0X2 — Services (includes food service; also covers mortuary affairs and fitness/recreation, not culinary-only)

clusters.notes: Space Force has no organic culinary/food-service enlisted specialty; dining facilities at Space Force units are run by Air Force Services personnel or contract food service.

clusters.name: Music & Media
clusters.description: Military bands and musicians, and public affairs, broadcast, journalism, and visual-information roles that document and communicate military activities.
aptitude_signals: Plays an instrument at an audition-ready level, or writes/shoots photo-video/edits regularly
Comfortable performing or publishing work in front of an audience
Interested in storytelling, journalism, or media production
Has produced content (video, photography, writing, music) outside of school assignments
related_asvab_composites: Navy:no line score — per-rating subtest sums
USCG:no line score — per-rating subtest sums

Air Force: UNVERIFIED code — Band (audition-based program, not a standard AFSC accession)
Marine Corps: UNVERIFIED code — Musician (Marine Corps Band, audition-based)
Coast Guard: UNVERIFIED code — Musician (U.S. Coast Guard Band, audition-based)

clusters.notes: Military band positions across every branch that has one are typically audition-based accessions — the ASVAB line score still decides general enlistment eligibility, but the specific billet is won by audition/recording, not line score alone; this nuance isn't fully captured by the schema. Several exact codes in this cluster were not independently verified this session and are flagged UNVERIFIED rather than guessed. Space Force has no music/band program and no independently documented public-affairs SFSC of its own; public affairs support is provided by Air Force personnel.

clusters.name: Maritime & Nautical
clusters.description: Shipboard operations, navigation, deck seamanship, and small-boat/cutter operations.
aptitude_signals: Comfortable at sea, or has boating/sailing/fishing experience
Interested in navigation, weather, or how ships operate
Doesn't mind confined shared spaces and rotating shifts
Drawn to being on the water rather than at a desk
related_asvab_composites: Navy:no line score — per-rating subtest sums
USCG:no line score — per-rating subtest sums

Army: 88K — Watercraft Operator (small Army watercraft / logistics-over-the-shore fleet; not a broad career field)

clusters.notes: Air Force and Space Force have no maritime specialty at all. Marine Corps personnel embark aboard Navy ships but have no organic shipboard-seamanship MOS of their own — a Marine-track candidate interested in this cluster would need to look at the Navy instead. Army's watercraft field (88K) is real but small and logistics-focused, not a broad seamanship career path.

clusters.name: Intelligence & Analysis
clusters.description: Collecting, processing, and analyzing imagery, signals, and all-source information to support military decision-making.
aptitude_signals: Enjoys research, pattern-finding, and synthesizing information from multiple sources
Comfortable with ambiguity and incomplete information
Discreet and comfortable with rules around sensitive/classified material
Strong reading comprehension and writing
related_asvab_composites: Navy:no line score — per-rating subtest sums
USCG:no line score — per-rating subtest sums
clusters.notes: Security clearance eligibility (typically Secret or Top Secret/SCI) is the dominant qualifying requirement in this cluster across all branches — often more restrictive than the ASVAB line score itself. A disqualifying background-investigation issue can rule out this cluster even with a qualifying score.

clusters.name: Space & Satellite
clusters.description: Operating, maintaining, and defending satellite systems, space launch, missile warning, and space-domain-awareness systems.
aptitude_signals: Interested in astronomy, orbital mechanics, or how satellites work
Strong math/physics aptitude
Comfortable with sustained-attention console/monitoring work
Drawn to space as a subject, not just aviation

Air Force: UNVERIFIED code — Space Systems Operations (legacy AFSC 1C6X1; mission now primarily owned by Space Force)

clusters.notes: This is Space Force's core mission, yet its enlisted specialty list for space operations is narrow by design — DO NOT PAD IT. Exact current numeric SFSC codes could not be independently verified this session (the official spaceforce.mil SFSC crosswalk document returned HTTP 403 on fetch); specialty names are reported consistently across secondary sources but should be confirmed against a live spaceforce.mil or af.mil source before RUN SPECIALTIES publishes codes. CORRECTED 2026-07-12: this previously read that the Army, Navy, Marine Corps and Coast Guard have NO organic enlisted space-operations career field. That is no longer true of the Army. The Army established MOS 40D (Tactical Space Operations Specialist), its first enlisted space-specific MOS, EFFECTIVE 1 OCTOBER 2026 (source: army.mil, official). It is not yet an open accession path as of this retrieval date, so a reader cannot enlist into it today — say that plainly rather than listing it as available. The Army's officer space path (functional area FA40) is unchanged. Navy, Marine Corps and Coast Guard still have no organic enlisted space-operations career field.

clusters.name: Legal
clusters.description: Military justice, legal administration, and paralegal support for judge advocates — courts-martial, legal assistance, and administrative law.
aptitude_signals: Interested in law, rules, or how the justice system works
Strong reading/writing and attention to procedural detail
Comfortable with confidentiality and formal process
Has considered paralegal work or law school
related_asvab_composites: Navy:no line score — per-rating subtest sums
USCG:no line score — per-rating subtest sums

Coast Guard: YN — Yeoman (with legal specialty training; not a separate enlisted legal rating)

clusters.notes: Coast Guard does not have a dedicated enlisted legal/paralegal rating comparable to the other branches' 27D/LN/5J0X1/4421 — legal-administrative support falls to Yeomen with additional training, working alongside Coast Guard Judge Advocates. Space Force has no organic legal-enlisted specialty; supported by Air Force JAG/paralegal personnel.

sources.title: ASVAB Line Scores Explained: GT, ST, CL, MM and Every Composite — Battalion Duty
Air Force ASVAB Score: MAGE Requirements for Every AFSC — ASVAB Hero
All About Air Force ASVAB Composite Scores — LiveAbout
How Each Military Branch Computes ASVAB Line Scores — Dummies
Marine ASVAB Line Scores Explained: GT, EL, MM, CL — Boots and Utes
Navy Rating ASVAB Score Requirements — NavyCS
Coast Guard ASVAB Score: Requirements by Rating — ASVAB Hero
Understanding Your ASVAB Results — Official ASVAB Career Exploration Program (asvabprogram.com)
ENLISTED ONLY USSF SPECIALTY CODE CROSSWALK (official, fetch attempted, returned HTTP 403 — content not retrieved)

unverified: ASVAB line-score composite formulas (e.g. Army GT = VE+AR, Air Force/Space Force MAGE formulas) were cross-checked across multiple independent secondary/aggregator sources for consistency but were NOT confirmed against a primary DoD/.mil ASVAB document within this session's 8-call tool budget, which per the task was allocated to grounding the composite systems. The structural facts (Army's 10 named line scores, AF/SF's MAGE, USMC's GT/EL/MM, Navy/USCG having no line scores) are corroborated across several independent sources and match the task's own binding framing, but exact formula weightings should be treated as directional pending primary-source confirmation.

All specialty codes (MOS/Rating/AFSC/SFSC) in example_specialties_by_branch are drawn from trained knowledge of long-stable, widely-documented occupational code lists, not independently re-fetched from a live .mil source in this session. Full per-specialty verification is deferred to the downstream RUN SPECIALTIES command (per-cluster tool budget of 15).

All Space Force (SFSC) numeric codes are explicitly flagged UNVERIFIED — the official spaceforce.mil enlisted specialty crosswalk PDF could not be fetched (HTTP 403), and public SFSC numbering has reportedly been renumbered multiple times since 2020.

Marine Corps cyber MOS codes (1710, 1721), Marine Corps Band/Combat Correspondent codes, and Coast Guard Band rating code were not confirmed against an official source this session and are marked UNVERIFIED code in place of a guessed number.

Air Force codes 6F0X1 (Financial Management), 3N0X2 (Public Affairs), 3E4X1 (Water and Fuels Systems Maintenance), 2A6X1 (Aerospace Propulsion), 2S0X1 (Logistics Plans), 2T2X1 (Air Transportation) reflect moderate-confidence trained knowledge, not a live .mil confirmation this session.
completion_accountability: 14 of 14 candidate clusters completed (technology-cyber, healthcare-medical, aviation, mechanical-engineering, law-enforcement-security, logistics-supply, combat-arms, business-admin, culinary, music-media, maritime-nautical, intelligence-analysis, space-satellite, legal). 0 clusters skipped. Within each cluster, every one of the 6 branches was explicitly addressed — populated with real specialties where the branch has genuine presence, or an explicit empty array plus a reasoned note where it does not (documented gaps: Marine Corps and Space Force in healthcare-medical; Space Force in aviation, law-enforcement-security, culinary, music-media, maritime-nautical, legal, and mechanical-engineering; Navy/Air Force/Space Force/Coast Guard in combat-arms; Air Force/Marine Corps/Space Force/Coast Guard in space-satellite; Air Force/Marine Corps/Space Force in maritime-nautical). Tool budget: 8 of 8 web search/fetch calls used, per the command's stated budget, concentrated on grounding the ASVAB composite systems (Army 10-line-score model, MAGE, USMC GT/EL/MM, Navy/USCG no-line-score model) as instructed; one WebFetch (official Space Force SFSC crosswalk) failed with HTTP 403 and its content could not be retrieved. Specialty-code-level verification against primary .mil sources is deferred to RUN SPECIALTIES per cluster, which has a larger per-cluster budget (15 calls) suited to that depth.
